title: 10 Herbal Remedies for Chills and Sore Throats
intro_desc: Herbal teas for chills are a natural remedy that helps to alleviate the discomfort and flu-like symptoms associated with chills. These teas work by boosting the immune system, reducing inflammation, and providing relief from congestion and fever. The best herbal teas for chills include Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Eucalyptus globulus, Ginkgo biloba, Sambucus nigra, Thymus vulgaris, Achillea millefolium, Rosmarinus officinalis, and Ulmus rubra. If left untreated, chills can lead to fatigue, decreased productivity, and a significant impact on daily life, causing people to miss work, school, and social events, ultimately affecting their overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that can help with chills. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce body temperature. When we have a fever or are feeling chilled, our body is fighting off an infection. The anti-inflammatory properties in Echinacea purpurea tea can help to calm down the body's defenses, which can make us feel better. It can also help to boost our immune system, which can prevent us from getting sick in the first place. Some people also use Echinacea purpurea tea to help with other symptoms of illness, such as sore throats and coughs. This is because it can help to reduce swelling and fight off the infection that is causing the symptoms. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and soothe the body, making it easier to manage chills.
Polyphenols are good for chills because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and ease cold symptoms by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Alkylamides are good for chills because they have anti-inflammatory and warming properties that help to increase blood circulation and reduce muscle tension, thereby alleviating feelings of cold and discomfort.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for chills
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal teas that help with chills, first you'll need to gather the right ingredients. You can find dried Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in most health food stores or online. Take one teaspoon of dried Achillea millefolium and add it to a cup of boiling water. Next, reduce the heat to a simmer for about 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to steep and release their medicinal properties. After the steeping time is up, strain the tea into another cup using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth to catch the solids. You can add a bit of honey to taste if you prefer your tea sweeter. Finally, drink the tea while it's warm to help alleviate chills and other symptoms. It's recommended to drink one to two cups of Achillea millefolium tea per day for optimal benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for centuries to help with chills. When we have chills, our body temperature drops, and we feel cold and shivery. Ginger tea can help to raise our body temperature and make us feel warmer. It does this by increasing the flow of blood to our skin, which helps to release the cold feeling. Ginger also contains a natural chemical that helps to reduce inflammation and fight off infections that can cause chills. Drinking ginger tea can also help to calm our stomach and soothe a cough or sore throat that can come with chills. Additionally, ginger has antiviral properties that can help to fight off the underlying cause of chills, such as a cold or flu virus.

Shogaol is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce body temperature and relieve cold and flu symptoms by blocking the production of a chemical that causes fever.

Shogaols are good for chills because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce body temperature and relieve cold symptoms.
Curcumenol is good for chills because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and soothe the body, making it easier to shake off the chill.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for chills
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea to help with chills, start by gathering dried or fresh leaves and flowers of the plant, also known as Yarrow. If using fresh, gently dry them in a warm place, away from direct sunlight. Next, measure one to two teaspoons of the dried leaves and flowers for every cup of boiling water you'll be using. Pour the boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for five to seven minutes, allowing the flavors and medicinal properties to infuse. Strain the liquid and discard the solids, then drink the tea hot or let it cool to your preferred temperature. You can also add honey or lemon to taste. As a general guideline, consume one to three cups per day to help alleviate chills and other symptoms associated with colds and flu.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a popular remedy for chills. The main reason is that it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the body. When you have a chill, your body produces excess mucus, which can make it hard to breathe and feel uncomfortable. Eucalyptus globulus contains a compound called eucalyptol, which has natural decongestant properties. This means it can help to break down the mucus and make it easier to cough up. As a result, you may feel relief from congestion and a chill. Drinking eucalyptus globulus tea may also help to reduce fever and soothe a sore throat. Additionally, the tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help to fight off underlying infections that may be causing the chill.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins are good for chills because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of fever and chills.
Quercetin is good for chills because it helps reduce inflammation and fight off infections in the body, making you feel warmer and more comfortable.
Tannins are good for chills because they help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections that cause fever, making them a natural remedy for soothing cold symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for chills
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea to help with chills, start by gathering a handful of dried yarrow flowers and leaves. If you don't have dried yarrow, you can dry fresh ones by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once you have the dried yarrow, combine it with one cup of boiling water in a teapot. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and medicinal properties to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the plant material. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to start with a small amount as yarrow can be quite bitter. Drink the tea warm to help soothe your body and reduce chills. You can make a larger batch of tea and store it in the fridge for up to a day, reheating it as needed. It's also essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using yarrow tea, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, is a popular herbal tea for treating chills. The tea contains glycyrrhizin, a natural compound that helps to warm up the body and fight off infection. When you drink Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, it increases the production of hormones that help to regulate body temperature. This makes you feel warmer and more comfortable, especially when you're feeling cold or have a low fever. Additionally, licorice root tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the body, which can be caused by chills. The tea also boosts your immune system, helping your body to fight off the underlying cause of the chills. By drinking Glycyrrhiza glabra tea regularly, you may be able to reduce the frequency and severity of chills, and feel more energized and healthy.

If you don't have dried flowers, you can dry fresh ones by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry, dark place. Once you have dried yarrow flowers, measure one teaspoon of them for every cup of boiling water you plan to use. Place the dried flowers in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup, and pour boiling water over them. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and medicinal properties to release. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the yarrow flowers. You can add honey to taste, but be careful not to add too much as yarrow can have a bitter taste. Drink the tea hot to help reduce chills and soothe your body.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is good for chills because it helps the body fight off colds and flu. The tea contains special compounds that boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off infections. Ginkgo biloba also has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease congestion in the chest and sinuses, making it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful when you have a cold or flu and are feeling stuffy or congested. The tea can also help to improve circulation, which can help to warm the body up and reduce the feeling of chilliness. Additionally, ginkgo biloba has antioxidant properties that help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can help to prevent illness and promote overall health. Drinking ginkgo biloba tea regularly may also help to reduce the severity and frequency of chills and other cold symptoms.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea to help with chills, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves and flowers of the plant. You can grow it in your garden or purchase it from a health food store. Rinse the leaves and flowers with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. For a cup of tea, use about one tablespoon of the plant material and place it in a teapot or infuser. Next, boil some water and pour it over the plant material, letting it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and properties to release. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the plant material. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to use a small amount as the tea is quite potent. Once the tea is ready, drink it as needed to help alleviate chills and soothe your body. It's also essential to note that Achillea millefolium tea should not be taken by pregnant women or people with certain medical conditions, so consult with a healthcare professional before using it.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tea is good for chills because it contains a medicine called quinine. Quinine helps to fight off a type of germ called the malaria parasite, which can cause chills. When you drink Cinchona officinalis tea, the quinine goes into your body and starts to work. It helps to lower your body temperature, so you don't feel so cold and shivery. Quinine also helps to make your blood vessels wider, which brings more blood to your skin and helps to warm you up. This tea has been used for a long time to help people feel better when they have a fever or are feeling cold and tired.

Place the yarrow in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup, and pour the boiling water over it. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has cooled slightly. Strain the tea into a separate container, and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to check with a healthcare professional before consuming any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are pregnant/breastfeeding. Once the tea has cooled, you can drink it as needed to help relieve chills and other symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tea, also known as thyme tea, is a natural remedy that can help to reduce chills. Thyme contains compounds that have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help to fight off infections that cause chills. When you drink thyme tea, these compounds are released into your body and can help to soothe a sore throat and calm your body. Thyme also contains a type of antioxidant called flavonoids, which can help to reduce inflammation and fever. Drinking thyme tea may also help to boost your immune system, which can help your body to fight off infections more effectively. Additionally, thyme tea has a warming effect on the body, which can help to relieve chills and make you feel more comfortable.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal teas that help with chills, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried Yarrow flowers and leaves from the plant. If using fresh Yarrow, gently rinse the leaves and flowers with clean water, and pat them dry with a paper towel. If using dried Yarrow, skip this step. Next, place the Yarrow in a clean tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Add boiling water to the Yarrow, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on your preference for the strength of the tea. Strain the tea into another cup, and discard the Yarrow leaves and flowers. You can add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate chills and other symptoms. Keep in mind that Yarrow tea is not recommended for pregnant or breastfeeding women, as well as people with kidney or liver problems.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elderberry, has been used for centuries to help with chills. The tea made from its berries is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties. When we're feeling cold, our bodies try to fight off an infection or virus. Sambucus nigra helps to reduce the severity of these symptoms by blocking the replication of the virus and reducing inflammation in the body. This means that the tea can help to relieve headaches, sore throats, and body aches that often come with chills. Additionally, elderberry has antiviral properties that help to boost our immune system, making it easier for our bodies to fight off the infection. By drinking Sambucus nigra tea, we can give our bodies a natural boost to help us feel better and recover faster.

remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal teas for chills
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea that helps with chills, you will need some fresh or dried Yarrow flowers. First, start by rinsing the flowers under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris. If using fresh flowers, chop them into smaller pieces to release their oils. Next, place one teaspoon of the chopped flowers into a heat-resistant cup or teapot. If you are using dried flowers, you can add one tablespoon instead. Pour in boiling water and let it steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the active ingredients to be released. Strain the tea into another cup using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the flowers. Let the tea cool down for a few minutes, then drink it while it's still warm. You can also add honey or lemon to taste if you prefer. Drinking Yarrow tea regularly may help to reduce chills and soothe your body, but it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis tea, also known as rosemary tea, is a good remedy for chills. Rosemary is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues. When made into a tea, it releases its active ingredients that can help to warm the body and fight off the cold. The tea's decongestant properties can help to clear the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion. This can be especially helpful when you have a cold or flu and are experiencing a fever or chills. Additionally, rosemary tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the body's response to illness, making it easier to recover. By drinking rosemary tea, you may be able to help your body regulate its temperature and feel more comfortable when you're experiencing chills. Rosemary tea is also easy to make, simply by steeping the leaves in hot water, making it a convenient and natural remedy for chills.

remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal teas for chills
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tea to help with chills, start by gathering a handful of dried or fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers. You can find this herb in most health food stores or grow it yourself in your garden. Rinse the leaves and flowers with clean water, then chop them into smaller pieces. Next, combine 1 tablespoon of the chopped herb with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help release the active ingredients. Strain the tea into a separate cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth, and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming the tea, especially if you have any underlying health conditions. Once the tea is ready, you can drink it hot or iced, depending on your preference. Drinking 1-2 cups of Achillea millefolium tea per day may help alleviate chills and promote a sense of comfort and well-being.

supplementary_best: To remedy chills, a combination of Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, and Eucalyptus globulus is effective. Echinacea purpurea helps boost the immune system, fighting off the underlying infection that causes the chills. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce fever and ease the discomfort associated with chills. Eucalyptus globulus has decongestant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, allowing the body to breathe easier. This combination works well together because it addresses multiple aspects of chills, from the immune system to the respiratory system.
supplementary_avoid: If you have chills, it's best to avoid herbal teas that can cause your body temperature to drop further or make you feel even more tired. One such tea is 'Valeriana officinalis', which is known for its sedative properties. Drinking it can make you feel drowsy and sleepy, which may worsen your chills. Another tea to avoid is 'Passiflora incarnata', as it also has a sedative effect and can make you feel more lethargic. 'Citrus aurantium' or bitter orange tea can also be avoided, as it can lower your body temperature and make you feel even more chilled. Additionally, 'Verbena officinalis' tea is not recommended as it can cause a decrease in body temperature and make you feel tired. These teas are best avoided when you have chills, as they can make your symptoms worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: When you're feeling cold and shivery, there are some natural remedies that can help. One of these remedies is taking a warm bath or shower, which can help raise your body temperature and make you feel better. This is because when your body temperature drops, your blood vessels constrict, making it harder for your body to warm up. By increasing blood flow to the skin, a warm bath or shower can help your body warm up more quickly.  Another remedy that can help with chills is applying heat directly to the body. You can do this by using a hot water bottle or a heating pad, which can be placed on your stomach or chest to help warm you up. This can be especially helpful if you're feeling cold and shivery due to a cold or the flu.  Some people also find that drinking apple cider vinegar can help with chills. Apple cider vinegar has antimicrobial properties, which can help your body fight off infections that can cause chills. Additionally, it has a natural warming effect that can help raise your body temperature.  Another remedy that can help with chills is taking a warm drink, such as broth or soup. These drinks can help raise your body temperature and provide essential nutrients that can help your body fight off infections. They can also be soothing and comforting, which can help you feel better when you're feeling cold and shivery.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have chills often experience other ailments like fever, headache, and body aches. These symptoms can be relieved with medicinal herbs like willow bark, which contains salicin, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin. Willow bark can help reduce fever and alleviate headaches.  Another herb that can help is ginger, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease body aches and reduce nausea. Ginger can also help with digestion issues, which may be caused by the fever or chills.  Elderberry is an herb that can help with fever and chills by reducing the severity of flu and cold symptoms. Its antiviral properties can help fight off the underlying infection.  Slippery elm can help soothe a sore throat, which is often caused by a fever or chills. Its demulcent properties can create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort.  Lastly, Echinacea can help boost the immune system, which can fight off the infection causing the chills and fever. Its antioxidant properties can also help reduce oxidative stress, which can exacerbate the symptoms.
